task_completion.description: Check if the cities shapefile is loaded into the gvSIG project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Table of Contents (TOC) on the left side of the screen lists 'ne_110m_populated_places', or the points are visible in the map view.

task_completion.description: Check if the rivers shapefile is loaded into the gvSIG project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Table of Contents (TOC) lists 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines', or the river lines are visible in the map view.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent opened the geoprocessing tools in gvSIG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A geoprocessing toolbox or specific distance calculation dialog (e.g., 'Distance', 'Spatial Join', or 'Nearest feature') is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Check if the distance tool is configured with the correct input layers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The geoprocessing dialog shows 'ne_110m_populated_places' as the input/source layer and 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines' as the reference/target layer for the distance calculation.

task_completion.description: Check if the resulting layer contains both city attributes and distance values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute table for the newly generated layer is open, displaying original city columns (like name, pop_max) alongside a new column representing the calculated distance.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent exports the result to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog in gvSIG is visible with the file path explicitly set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/cities_river_dist.shp'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the gvSIG GUI for the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The gvSIG application window is visible and interacted with. The agent did not use a terminal with ogr2ogr, Python, or other CLI tools to bypass gvSIG.

integrity.description: Verify the geoprocessing tool was actually executed within the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the tool running (e.g., progress bar, log messages) or the new layer appearing in the TOC as a direct result of the tool, rather than manual file manipulation or text editing.

integrity.description: Verify the export was performed through gvSIG's interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export/save dialog belongs to the gvSIG application, rather than a terminal copy command (cp) or a script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified paths: Cities at /home/ga/gvsig_data/cities/ne_110m_populated_places.shp, Rivers at /home/ga/gvsig_data/rivers/ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines.shp, Output at /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/cities_river_dist.shp. The cities layer contains 243 features. Note: Due to the coarse 1:110m scale of the dataset, some cities (e.g., London) will have large calculated distances to the nearest river (approx 9.026 degrees). Do not penalize the agent for large distance values as long as the geoprocessing tool was configured and executed correctly.
